identity.name: Hvar
identity.description: Hvar is a Croatian island in the Adriatic Sea, located off the Dalmatian coast, lying between the islands of Brač, Vis and Korčula. Approximately 68 kilometres (42.25 mi) long, with a high east–west ridge of Mesozoic limestone and dolomite, the island of Hvar is unusual in the area for having a large fertile coastal plain, and fresh water springs. Its hillsides are covered in pine forests, with vineyards, olive groves, fruit orchards and lavender fields in the agricultural areas.

osm_tags.description: the restaurant's offer is based on meals prepared using local groceries: the fish and seafood caught in the local sea and vegetables cultivated on the island.
Experience Passarola in the heart of Hvar. A charming rooftop restaurant offering a modern take on Dalmatian classics.

osm_tags.note: Dinner is available by prior reservation, at least one day in advance. Without a reservation, we offer a daily menu.
